title: Add pre-commit skill-sync hook
description: Update the root pre-commit config so it enforces `python3 tools/skills/sync_selected_skills.py --check` and matches the README.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Pre-commit runs the selected-skill sync check | - [x] README and pre-commit behavior match

title: Expand PR workflow path coverage
description: Update the PR review workflow path filters so root docs such as `README.md` and `CONTRIBUTING.md` trigger validation and review.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Root docs changes trigger the workflow | - [x] Workflow scope matches repo reality

title: Split validation from AI PR review
description: Create a dedicated validation workflow for push and pull_request status checks, leaving AI review as a separate PR-focused workflow.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Deterministic validation runs on default-branch pushes | - [x] README badges and workflow purpose are clearer

title: Add contribution templates
description: Add lightweight GitHub pull request and issue templates that fit the repo's current informal contribution process.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] PR template exists | - [x] At least one issue template exists | - [x] Templates match the current contribution style

title: Fix Gemini action auth input
description: Update the Gemini CLI workflow step to use the correct `gemini_api_key` action input so the configured repository secret is actually passed through.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Workflow uses the correct action input name | - [x] Gemini CLI can receive the configured repository secret

title: Replace Gemini action wrapper
description: Replace the unsupported Gemini GitHub Action wrapper usage with an explicit CLI run that reads the generated prompt file and posts the result to the PR separately.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Workflow reads the generated prompt file directly | - [x] Gemini output is summarized and posted separately to the PR | - [x] Runner creates the Gemini state directory before invoking the CLI

title: Define stack doc and skill split
description: Document the chosen pattern: stack skills are the primary deep agent-facing source, `skills/<stack>/references/` holds specifics, stack agent files stay thin, and stack spec docs define project-template conventions while remaining standalone.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Canonical responsibilities for docs, skills, and AGENTS files are documented | - [x] Required file layout and naming conventions are defined | - [x] Rollout constraints for standalone copied templates are explicit

title: Refactor Next.js stack to skill-first guidance
description: Create a single repo-owned `nextjs` skill with deep references, slim the Next.js agent instruction triplet into routing and always-hot guardrails, and reduce `specs/nextjs/docs/` to concise standalone project guidance.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] `skills/nextjs/SKILL.md` defines trigger rules, workflow, and targeted reference loading | - [x] `skills/nextjs/references/` covers the deep Next.js specifics needed for implementation and review | - [x] `specs/nextjs` agent files route to the skill and keep only minimal always-hot rules | - [x] `specs/nextjs/docs/` remains useful as a standalone, thinner template doc set

title: Validate stack skill rollout and standalone templates
description: Validate the new stack skills, keep selected-skill mirroring aligned, and confirm each stack spec remains coherent and safe when copied without repo-local skills.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Repo-owned stack skills are selected and mirrored consistently when introduced | - [ ] Agent file and template validators pass after the rollout | - [ ] Each stack spec remains understandable and safe as a standalone template without repo-local skills
